time_window: Wave 1 (npm atomic-lockfile): malicious commits live on 2026-06-11 (one infected package, exodus-wallet-bin, was only ~4 hours old at 21:21 CEST); Arch staff began resetting commits from ~19:47 CEST and had checked all thread reports by 23:47 CEST 2026-06-11, with stragglers fixed into 2026-06-12. Wave 2 (bun js-digest): commits pushed within the ~10 hours before 2026-06-12 15:03 CEST (roughly 05:00-15:00 CEST June 12) and still ongoing at thread capture time.

Not stated precisely. Article (published 12 June 2026, 06:39 AM EDT) says "Since yesterday Arch Linux maintainers have been working to reset/delete all of the malicious content", implying malicious commits were live until at least 2026-06-11; start date of malicious commits not given by this source.

Not explicitly stated. Incident first reported on the aur-general mailing list; article published 2026-06-11 21:32 UTC with cleanup (commit removal, account bans) still ongoing and the affected-package list still changing.

Assigned source (earlier 2025 incident): malicious packages live from 2025-07-16 ~20:00 UTC+2 until deletion 2025-07-18 ~18:00 UTC+2. 2026 Atomic Arch incident window not covered by this source; other sources date it ~2026-06-11.

Report dated 2026-06-11. Editor's note states "Many More AUR packages were attacked today in the same way" (i.e., on/around 2026-06-11). No precise start/end timestamp for malicious commits is given in this source.
Not stated in this source. Blog published 2026-06-11; Sonatype says "Analysis of the campaign is ongoing" and the affected-package count "may grow as orphaned packages continue to be audited."

Malicious commits went live ~2026-06-11 and continued in waves through 2026-06-12 (cleanup ongoing). atomic-lockfile npm pkg uploaded ~2026-06-10. First (npm/atomic-lockfile) wave: oldest affected pkg sshuttlee-bin, newest python-rembg per DodoGTA. Second wave (bun + js-digest/axios) began 2026-06-12.</time_window>
<parameter name="official_guidance">Jonathan Grotelueschen (tippfehlr, Arch staff), AUR REPORT THREAD 2026-06-11: "we're working hard to reset/delete all malicious commits and ban the accounts. If you find more malicious packages, please **send them as a reply to this email** to keep them all in one thread." He later clarified "The commits impersonate the previous commit's name and email." kpcyrd (Arch staff): "The pattern is always the same, `npm install something` in post_install which in turn has a package.json with ... `\"preinstall\": \"./src/hooks/deps\"` ... Which is an ELF executable. I've also notified npmjs.com that atomic-lockfile is malware." Rolled offending repos back to the prior commit; another moderator suspended the uploading account. BBS forum admin: "The Moderation team is aware and a few of the moderators are already cleaning things up!"

Malicious commits dated 2026-06-11 (e.g. gnome-randr-rust commit da9f4cf2... on Jun 11, 2026); first reported 2026-06-11; reported still ongoing/being audited as of 2026-06-12 12:58 UTC. No precise start/end timestamp published.

List was generated with `git log --all --since="48 hours ago" -p -S atomic-lockfile --decorate=full` over an AUR git mirror, so the malicious commits landed within ~48h before list creation, i.e. roughly 2026-06-10 through 2026-06-12 (first public reports 2026-06-11). Per the Arch forum thread, npm replaced the atomic-lockfile package with a security placeholder around 12:00 UTC on the day of the discussion.

npm-side, per Socket timestamps: atomic-lockfile@1.4.2 published 2026-06-10 15:05:45 UTC, Socket-detected 2026-06-10 15:14:53 UTC; js-digest@4.2.2 published 2026-06-12 10:21:34 UTC, Socket-detected 2026-06-12 10:27:38 UTC. Both later unpublished. Page does not state the AUR PKGBUILD commit window.

Wave 1 (npm install atomic-lockfile): malicious commits 2026-06-10T15:21:29Z to 2026-06-11T17:19:26Z UTC. Wave 2 (bun add js-digest/lockfile-js): 2026-06-12T12:10:16Z to 2026-06-12T13:35:02Z UTC. Arch staff force-push resets ran 2026-06-10T16:47:05Z to 2026-06-12T14:07:54Z UTC (ongoing at time of collection).

First wave: malicious commits landed within ~24h before first reports on 2026-06-11 (forum mod: "recent is in the last week"). Second wave (bun-based) reported 2026-06-12. melindajuhasz's 13 packages last modified 2026-06-12 12:43-12:56 UTC (timestamps may reflect attacker pushes or staff resets in progress).

Malicious commits live ~2026-06-11 into 2026-06-12; janosjonas batch pushes timestamped 2026-06-12 ~12:54 UTC (sequential ~5s apart). atomic-lockfile npm pkg replaced with security placeholder ~12:00 UTC 2026-06-12; js-digest unpublished by 2026-06-12.

Campaign first reported 2026-06-11 (ioctl.fail report date; forum says commits were recent/within days). For this account's 13 packages, staff force-reset pushes landed 2026-06-12 12:50:21Z to 12:52:39Z, so malicious commits were live until then (roughly a 1-day window). Exact malicious push times not recoverable from AUR git (branches already reset); recover from GitHub mirror force-push activity log.

Malicious commits pushed within the week before the 2026-06-11 report (forum: "recent is in the last week"). Observed AUR force-push/reset wave on 2026-06-11 ~20:51-21:39 UTC (staff cleanup of compromised packages); 4 packages were still live or re-compromised on 2026-06-12 (12:20-13:46 UTC).

Malicious commits pushed ~2026-06-11 ("within the last 24 hours" per post #1 at 2026-06-12T00:12Z); confirmed example commit timestamp 2026-06-11T13:54:56Z. cscs checker treats installs dated Jun 9-12 2026 ("within 48 hours of the campaign") as suspect.
